steps.label: Conduct data & AI research
steps.prompt:
### STEP 0 - SKIP CHECK (run this BEFORE anything else in this step)
Run this PowerShell exactly:
```powershell
$today = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd'
$folder = '__BRIEFINGS_FOLDER__\Weekly'
$cutoff = (Get-Date).AddMinutes(-10)
$existing = Get-ChildItem -Path $folder -Filter "$today*.md" -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.LastWriteTime -lt $cutoff } | Select-Object -First 1
if ($existing) { "SKIP: $($existing.FullName)" } else { "PROCEED" }
```
If output starts with SKIP:, emit ONLY this line and END the step: Skipped: Data SE Weekly Briefing for <today> already exists at <full path from SKIP line>.
If output is PROCEED, continue.

CRITICAL OUTPUT CONTRACT - READ BEFORE USING TOOLS:
- This step MUST end with a SHORT, non-empty PLAIN TEXT final response (one line). Never end the step with a tool call, raw tool result, JSON object/array, or any structured object the runner could render as [object Object].
- The detailed research is passed to the next step via a HANDOFF FILE (see PERSIST section), NOT inline. Do NOT paste the full bullet list as your final response - a large step-1 result is what gets stored as [object Object].
- If the skip check returns SKIP, the final response must be exactly the one-line Skipped message above.
- If research cannot complete, return plain text beginning with `Research failed:` and the concise reason.

### STEP 1 - Research
I am a Microsoft Data Solutions Engineer. Rank coverage by these interests, in order:
1. Microsoft Fabric & Power BI (OneLake, Warehouse, Lakehouse, Data Factory, Real-Time Intelligence, Spark/notebooks, capacities, Direct Lake, semantic models, GA & preview features)
2. Azure Data Platform (Azure Databricks, Synapse, Azure SQL/SQL Server, Cosmos DB, Data Factory, Purview/governance, Fabric-Databricks interop)
3. Data + AI integration (AI Foundry on your data, Copilot in Fabric & Power BI, Fabric Data Agents, RAG over enterprise data, vector search, Lakehouse-to-LLM patterns)
4. Broader data ecosystem & industry news (Databricks, Snowflake, dbt, Confluent/Kafka, DuckDB, Iceberg/Delta/Parquet, open table formats, launches, funding, M&A, exec moves)
5. Lighter AI dev-tools watch (GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, MCP, agentic workflows - keep brief)
Skip: academic ML research, papers, benchmarks unless tied to a shipped data/analytics product.

TOOLING NOTE (IMPORTANT - this environment has NO firecrawl_search / web_search / firecrawl_scrape tools). Use the web_fetch tool only, with this proven two-stage pattern. Cap total web_fetch calls at ~25.

(A) DISCOVERY via Google News RSS - call web_fetch with raw=true and parse each <item> (title, <source>, <pubDate>):
  https://news.google.com/rss/search?q=<URL-ENCODED QUERY>%20when%3a7d&hl=en-US&gl=US&ceid=US:en
  Use when:7d for a 7-day lookback window; widen to when:14d only if results are thin. Parallelize these 8 queries:
   1. "Microsoft Fabric"
   2. "Power BI" OR "Direct Lake" OR "semantic model"
   3. "Azure Databricks" OR Synapse OR "Azure SQL" OR "Microsoft Purview"
   4. Databricks OR Snowflake OR dbt OR Confluent
   5. "Apache Iceberg" OR "Delta Lake" OR "open table format" OR DuckDB
   6. "Copilot in Fabric" OR "Fabric Data Agent" OR "AI Foundry"
   7. Azure data platform OR analytics OR governance announcement
   8. "Claude Code" OR MCP OR "agentic coding" OR "GitHub Copilot"

(B) CANONICAL URL RECOVERY - Google News RSS item links are redirects (news.google.com/rss/articles/...), NOT canonical; never pass them forward. For each high-signal story, recover the real article URL via DuckDuckGo Lite and read the uddg= parameter of the first matching result (URL-DECODE it):
  https://lite.duckduckgo.com/lite/?q=<URL-ENCODED exact headline + publisher>
  Prefer the official vendor/publisher domain; add a site: filter (e.g. site:azure.microsoft.com) when a mirror outranks the original.
  DO NOT use html.duckduckgo.com (hits a CAPTCHA) or bing.com/news (returns an empty body) - neither works in this environment.

=== URL CAPTURE PROTOCOL (MANDATORY) ===
For EVERY story capture a CANONICAL ARTICLE URL (deep link with a real slug), taken verbatim from the URL-decoded uddg= value - do NOT shorten, normalize, or guess. Forbidden URL shapes (never pass forward): Google News redirect links; bare-domain root; section root (/blog/, /news/, /en-us/blog/, /ai/, /press/); search pages (/search, ?s=, ?q=); paths with <2 segments where the last is <6 chars. If you cannot recover a valid canonical URL for a story, DROP it.

=== PERSIST RESULTS TO A HANDOFF FILE (MANDATORY) ===
1. Compute $today = yyyy-MM-dd. Ensure the handoff folder exists:
   __BRIEFINGS_FOLDER__\Weekly\.handoff
   (create it with New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force if missing).
2. Write the FULL research bullet list to the handoff file (overwrite if it exists):
   __BRIEFINGS_FOLDER__\Weekly\.handoff\research-<today>.md
   Use this exact bullet format inside the file, one bullet per valid story:
   - headline: <headline>
     canonical_url: <valid deep article URL>
     publisher: <publisher>
     why_matters: <one concise sentence for a Microsoft Data SE>
   Use the create/edit file tools or PowerShell Set-Content with -Encoding UTF8. Verify the file exists and is > 0 bytes.
3. FINAL RESPONSE (MANDATORY, SHORT, PLAIN TEXT - exactly one line, do NOT paste the bullets):
   Research complete: <N> stories saved to __BRIEFINGS_FOLDER__\Weekly\.handoff\research-<today>.md. PROCEED to curation.
   If the file write fails, instead return one line: Research failed: could not write handoff file - <reason>.

steps.label: Curate data news
steps.prompt:
### STEP 0 - SKIP CHECK (run this BEFORE anything else in this step)
Run this PowerShell exactly:
```powershell
$today = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd'
$folder = '__BRIEFINGS_FOLDER__\Weekly'
$cutoff = (Get-Date).AddMinutes(-10)
$existing = Get-ChildItem -Path $folder -Filter "$today*.md" -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.LastWriteTime -lt $cutoff } | Select-Object -First 1
if ($existing) { "SKIP: $($existing.FullName)" } else { "PROCEED" }
```
- If output starts with `SKIP:`, emit ONLY this line and END the step: `Skipped: Data SE Weekly Briefing for <today> already exists at <full path from SKIP line>.`
- If output is `PROCEED`, continue.

=== LOAD RESEARCH FROM HANDOFF FILE (MANDATORY) ===
Step 1 now passes its results via a file, not inline. Before curating, READ this file to get the {headline, canonical_url, publisher, why_matters} bullets:
  __BRIEFINGS_FOLDER__\Weekly\.handoff\research-<today>.md  (where <today> = yyyy-MM-dd)
Use those bullets as your input. If that file is missing or empty, fall back to the "Output from previous step" text. Do NOT fail merely because the inline previous-step text is short (it is intentionally a one-line confirmation).

---
### STEP 2 - Curate
Curating for a Microsoft Data Solutions Engineer. Take the {headline, canonical_url, publisher, why_matters} tuples loaded above.
- Deduplicate (same event -> keep the most authoritative source, e.g. official Fabric/Databricks/Azure blog over a re-write).
- Filter out: ML research papers, benchmark-only stories, AI ethics op-eds, generic LLM hype with no shipped data/analytics product, crypto/web3 noise.
- Bucket each surviving story into exactly one section:
  1. Microsoft Fabric & Power BI
  2. Azure Data Platform (Databricks, Synapse, Azure SQL, Cosmos DB, Purview, ADF)
  3. Data + AI Integration (Copilot in Fabric, Fabric Data Agents, AI Foundry on data, RAG/vector)
  4. Broader Data Ecosystem (Snowflake, dbt, Confluent/Kafka, Iceberg/Delta, DuckDB, open table formats, funding/M&A/exec moves)
  5. AI Dev-Tools Watch (brief - Copilot, Claude Code, MCP, agentic coding)
- Rank within each section by: (a) relevance to a Microsoft Data SE, (b) recency, (c) actionability. Give extra weight to anything I could show in a Fabric or Power BI demo.
- Drop any story still missing a valid canonical_url.
Pass the curated, bucketed, ranked list forward to the write step.

steps.label: Write briefing report
steps.prompt:
### STEP 0 — SKIP CHECK (run this BEFORE anything else in this step)
Run this PowerShell exactly:
```powershell
$today = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd'
$folder = '__BRIEFINGS_FOLDER__\Weekly'
$cutoff = (Get-Date).AddMinutes(-10)
$existing = Get-ChildItem -Path $folder -Filter "$today*.md" -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.LastWriteTime -lt $cutoff } | Select-Object -First 1
if ($existing) { "SKIP: $($existing.FullName)" } else { "PROCEED" }
```
- If output starts with `SKIP:`, emit ONLY this line and END the step: `Skipped: Data SE Weekly Briefing for <today> already exists at <full path from SKIP line>.`
- If output is `PROCEED`, continue.

---
=== URL VALIDATION GATE (RUN BEFORE WRITING ANY BULLET) ===
For each candidate story, REJECT and DROP it if ANY of these are true:
- URL path is empty or exactly '/'
- URL path matches /^\/(blog|news|stories|en-us|articles?|press|ai|data|tech|posts|category|tag)\/?$/i
- URL has fewer than 2 non-empty path segments AND the last segment is < 6 chars
- URL is a search results page (path contains '/search' or query starts with ?s= or ?q=)
- URL is identical to another bullet's URL in the same section (duplicate)
A valid article URL has a meaningful slug. If a valuable story has no deep article URL, DO NOT include it. Better 3 great links than 8 broken ones.

---
### STEP 3 — Write the report

STYLE: Professional but scannable. Aim between a polished executive brief and a fast-skim bulletin.
- Lead each bullet with the bolded headline link, then a crisp "so what" — complete thought, but trimmed: no filler, no hedging, no marketing adjectives.
- One sentence per bullet is ideal; two only when the second adds real signal. Hard cap 35 words.
- Keep a professional, neutral voice (no slang, no hype, no emojis), but favor short, direct sentences over long formal ones.
- Executive Summary: 2–3 tight sentences, plain and confident.
- "What I'd Do Today" and "Account Opportunity Matches": short, action-first phrasing (start with a verb).

Generate a Markdown file with EXACTLY this structure and section ORDER (NO emojis anywhere — not in headers, bullets, or summary):

```markdown
# Data SE Weekly Briefing — {{YYYY-MM-DD}}

## Executive Summary
2–3 tight sentences. The "if you read nothing else" version. Lead with the single biggest story for a Microsoft Data SE. No more than 3 sentences.

## What I'd Do Today
3 concrete, action-first suggestions for me (Microsoft Data SE) based on the above — e.g., "Demo X in your next Fabric customer sync", "Try Y in a Lakehouse", "Spend time learning Z, it's gaining traction".

<!-- The "Account Opportunity Matches" section will be inserted here by the next step. Leave this position for it. -->

## Microsoft Fabric & Power BI
5–10 ranked bullets. Format each as:
- **[Headline](url)** — crisp "so what" for a Microsoft Data SE. Source: *publisher*.

## Azure Data Platform
5–10 ranked bullets, same format. Databricks, Synapse, Azure SQL, Cosmos DB, Purview, ADF.

## Data + AI Integration
5–10 ranked bullets, same format. Copilot in Fabric, Fabric Data Agents, AI Foundry on data, RAG/vector.

## Broader Data Ecosystem
5–10 ranked bullets, same format. Snowflake, dbt, Confluent, Iceberg/Delta, DuckDB, funding/M&A/exec moves.

## AI Dev-Tools Watch
3–5 ranked bullets, same format. Keep brief — Copilot, Claude Code, MCP, agentic coding.

---
*Generated {{TIMESTAMP}} by Data SE Weekly Briefing Agent. Sources: N articles across M domains.*
```

Rules:
- Every bullet must include a working source URL that PASSED the URL VALIDATION GATE above.
- Keep bullets ≤ 35 words.
- If a section has < 3 quality stories after validation, write "Slow news day in this section." rather than padding.
- No emojis anywhere. Plain markdown only.
- Save nothing in this step; pass the full report text forward (the Save step writes the file).

steps.label: Match news to accounts
steps.prompt:
### STEP 0 — SKIP CHECK (run this BEFORE anything else in this step)
Run this PowerShell exactly:
```powershell
$today = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd'
$folder = '__BRIEFINGS_FOLDER__\Weekly'
$cutoff = (Get-Date).AddMinutes(-10)
$existing = Get-ChildItem -Path $folder -Filter "$today*.md" -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.LastWriteTime -lt $cutoff } | Select-Object -First 1
if ($existing) { "SKIP: $($existing.FullName)" } else { "PROCEED" }
```
- If output starts with `SKIP:`, emit ONLY this line and END the step: `Skipped: Data SE Weekly Briefing for <today> already exists at <full path from SKIP line>.`
- If output is `PROCEED`, continue.

GUARDRAIL: This step ONLY produces recommendations for ME. Do NOT contact, email, or message any customer/account. No outbound messages to anyone except (later) my own email. All account data stays private to me.

---
### STEP 4 — Match news to my accounts
I am a Microsoft Data SE. These are the accounts I own (my book of business):
__ACCOUNTS__

Goal: take the top stories from the briefing (Steps 1–3) and decide which of my accounts would find them useful, with evidence from my own M365 signals.

Method (be efficient — cap total searches at ~25):
1. Select the 6–10 highest-impact stories from the briefing (prioritize Fabric/Power BI, Azure data platform, Data+AI integration, and anything I could demo).
2. PRIMARY — use the m365 tools directly; they are faster and give better, quotable signals than WorkIQ. For each story THEME (e.g. "Fabric Data Agents", "Direct Lake", "Iceberg/OneLake interop", "Purview governance", "Copilot in Power BI", "Databricks"), run `m365_search_emails` and `m365_search_chats` over ~the last 60 days with theme keywords, plus `m365_list_events` for upcoming meetings. Match hits to accounts by sender domain, attendee, or company name. A couple of broad theme keywords (e.g. "Fabric OR Power BI OR OneLake OR Copilot" and "Databricks OR Purview OR Iceberg OR governance") with startDate ~60 days back is usually enough.
3. OPTIONAL — WorkIQ, only for cross-service reasoning a direct search cannot answer. If used, accept the EULA once first: `$env:USERPROFILE\.copilot\bin\workiq.cmd accept-eula`. Keep WorkIQ queries NARROW (one theme at a time) — broad "all accounts x all themes" questions time out at the 300s limit.
4. Only record a match when there is a REAL signal (a quote, meeting topic, stated initiative, pain point, or upcoming QBR/roadmap discussion). Do NOT invent or assume interest. No-evidence = no match.

Build an "## Account Opportunity Matches" markdown section. Use short, action-first phrasing. For each match:
- **<Account>** — **[news headline](url)**
  - Why them: 1–2 sentences citing the M365 signal (e.g. "In their 5/28 QBR invite they flagged self-service analytics; their last email asked about Copilot in Power BI.").
  - Suggested play: concrete action starting with a verb (e.g. "Offer a 30-min Fabric Data Agents demo", "Send the Build announcement and propose a follow-up").
  - Confidence: High / Medium / Low.

Rank matches by confidence then account priority. If no account has a real signal for a story, omit it. If NO matches at all today, write: "No strong account matches surfaced from today's news."

Then INSERT this "## Account Opportunity Matches" section into the briefing report from Step 3, placed at the `<!-- ... -->` placeholder (immediately AFTER the "## What I'd Do Today" section and BEFORE the first news section "## Microsoft Fabric & Power BI"). Pass the full updated report forward to the Save step.

steps.label: Save report
steps.prompt:
### STEP 0 — SKIP CHECK (run this BEFORE anything else in this step)
Run this PowerShell exactly:
```powershell
$today = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd'
$folder = '__BRIEFINGS_FOLDER__\Weekly'
$cutoff = (Get-Date).AddMinutes(-10)
$existing = Get-ChildItem -Path $folder -Filter "$today*.md" -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.LastWriteTime -lt $cutoff } | Select-Object -First 1
if ($existing) { "SKIP: $($existing.FullName)" } else { "PROCEED" }
```
- If output starts with `SKIP:`, emit ONLY this line and END the step: `Skipped: Data SE Weekly Briefing for <today> already exists at <full path from SKIP line>.`
- If output is `PROCEED`, continue.

---
### STEP 5 — Save
Create the folder if it does not exist, then write the FULL report handed off from the previous step (the Step 3 briefing WITH the "Account Opportunity Matches" section appended) to:
`__BRIEFINGS_FOLDER__\Weekly\{{YYYY-MM-DD}}.md`

This is the WORKING filename. Step 6 renames it at the end of the run, appending up to 3 top story identifiers from the Executive Summary (e.g. `2026-06-05 -- Fabric RTI GA, Databricks Iceberg, dbt Fusion.md`).

steps.label: Convert, link & email
steps.prompt:
### STEP 0 — SKIP CHECK (run this BEFORE anything else in this step) Run this PowerShell exactly: ```powershell $today = Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd' $folder = '__BRIEFINGS_FOLDER__\Weekly' $cutoff = (Get-Date).AddMinutes(-10) $existing = Get-ChildItem -Path $folder -Filter "$today*.md" -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.LastWriteTime -lt $cutoff } | Select-Object -First 1 if ($existing) { "SKIP: $($existing.FullName)" } else { "PROCEED" } ``` - If output starts with `SKIP:`, emit ONLY this line and END the step: `Skipped: Data SE Weekly Briefing for already exists at .` - If output is `PROCEED`, continue. CRITICAL: This step MUST emit a non-empty PLAIN TEXT response. If you would finish with nothing, write at least a one-line status summary so the runner does not fail with "no response". --- ### STEP 6 — Convert, link & email Do each action and report status inline. #### 6a — RENAME the working .md Read the saved .md from Step 5 (working name `{{YYYY-MM-DD}}.md`). Extract up to 3 top story identifiers from the Executive Summary (in order of impact) — short product/company/release names (e.g. 'Fabric RTI GA', 'Databricks Iceberg', 'dbt Fusion'). Rules: - <= 3 identifiers, in order of impact. - Each identifier <= 30 chars after sanitization. Sanitize: strip \ / : * ? " < > | and emoji, collapse whitespace. - Total filename <= 100 chars (truncate last identifier with ellipsis if needed). - New filename: if Exec Summary missing -> '{{YYYY-MM-DD}} -- (no summary).md'; else '{{YYYY-MM-DD}} -- , , .md'. - Rename via PowerShell Rename-Item. Treat the renamed full path as canonical. - If rename fails: log it, keep the original working filename, do NOT fail the step. #### 6b — CONVERT the .md to a BRANDED, STYLED PDF Pandoc IS installed; if `pandoc` isn't on PATH use `$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Pandoc\pandoc.exe`. There is NO LaTeX/wkhtmltopdf engine and `npx md-to-pdf` is NOT available — the reliable path is pandoc md->styled HTML, then Edge prints the HTML. msedge.exe: 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft\Edge\Application\msedge.exe' (fall back to 'C:\Program Files\Microsoft\Edge\Application\msedge.exe'). Step 1 — write the theme CSS to a temp file $css (overwrite each run), EXACTLY: ```css :root{ --ink:#1f2329; --muted:#5b6470; --accent:#0b5cab; --accent-deep:#08305c; --rule:#e2e6ea; --chip:#eef4fb; --chip-border:#d7e3f2; } *{box-sizing:border-box;} html{-webkit-print-color-adjust:exact;print-color-adjust:exact;} body{font-family:'Segoe UI',system-ui,-apple-system,'Helvetica Neue',Arial,sans-serif;color:var(--ink);line-height:1.55;font-size:11pt;max-width:860px;margin:0 auto;padding:34px 54px 44px;} .ms-header{display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;margin:0 0 8px;} .ms-brand{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:9px;} .ms-word{font-size:15pt;font-weight:600;color:#3b3b3b;letter-spacing:.2px;} .ms-tag{font-size:9pt;color:#8a929c;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.13em;text-align:right;} .ms-stripe{height:4px;border-radius:2px;margin:6px 0 18px;background:linear-gradient(90deg,#F25022 0 25%,#7FBA00 0 25% 50%,#00A4EF 0 50% 75%,#FFB900 0 75%);} h1{font-size:24pt;font-weight:700;color:var(--accent-deep);margin:0 0 16px;letter-spacing:-.01em;border-bottom:3px solid var(--accent);padding-bottom:12px;} h2{font-size:14.5pt;font-weight:600;color:var(--accent-deep);margin:30px 0 10px;padding:7px 0 7px 13px;border-left:4px solid var(--accent);background:linear-gradient(90deg,var(--chip),transparent);} p{margin:8px 0;} a{color:var(--accent);text-decoration:none;border-bottom:1px solid rgba(11,92,171,.32);} ul{margin:8px 0;padding-left:22px;} li{margin:6px 0;} li>ul{margin-top:4px;} strong{color:var(--ink);font-weight:650;} hr{border:none;border-top:1px solid var(--rule);margin:28px 0 12px;} em{color:var(--muted);} hr + p{color:var(--muted);font-size:9.5pt;} h2:first-of-type + p{background:var(--chip);border:1px solid var(--chip-border);border-left:4px solid var(--accent);padding:14px 16px;border-radius:6px;font-size:11.5pt;} @page{margin:12mm;} @media print{ body{padding:0 6mm;} li,h2{break-inside:avoid;} .ms-header,.ms-stripe{break-inside:avoid;} } ``` Step 2 — write the brand header to a temp file $header (overwrite each run), EXACTLY: ```html
Microsoft
Data Solutions Engineering
Weekly Briefing
``` Step 3 — convert md to a self-contained styled HTML with the header injected: `& "" -s --embed-resources --css "" --include-before-body "
" -o ""` Step 4 — print that HTML to the final PDF (same base name as the .md, same folder): `& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft\Edge\Application\msedge.exe" --headless=new --disable-gpu --no-pdf-header-footer --print-to-pdf="" ("file:///" + ("" -replace '\\','/'))` then `Start-Sleep -Seconds 4` and delete the tmp html + css + header temp files. Confirm the .pdf exists and is > 0 bytes. If pandoc or Edge fails: log it and continue — fall back to linking the .md instead of the .pdf. #### 6c — GET a clickable OneDrive/SharePoint link Use the m365 OneDrive tools to locate the PDF (or the .md if conversion failed) by name and create/retrieve a VIEW sharing link. Capture it as $shareUrl. NOTE: Graph search can lag a few minutes behind a freshly written PDF — if `m365_search_files` does not find the new PDF yet, either retry after a short wait, or resolve the file's webUrl by name in the 'Data SE Briefings' folder. Only as a last resort use the local full path. The link MUST be a real https URL so it renders clickable in email. #### 6d — EMAIL the briefing to myself (clickable link) Get my own email address via `m365_get_my_profile`. Then send an email to that address with `m365_send_email`, isHtml = true: - to: my own email - subject: `Data SE Weekly Briefing — {{YYYY-MM-DD}}` - body (HTML): the Executive Summary (2–3 sentences, exactly as written in the file) in a `

`, then a clearly clickable link on its own line: `

Open the full briefing (PDF)

` Keep it minimal and clean — no emojis, no extra sections. Just the Exec Summary and the link. If the Executive Summary is missing/empty, send subject `Data SE Weekly Briefing — {{YYYY-MM-DD}} (incomplete)` and body `

Executive Summary not produced — check the file.

Open the briefing

`. #### 6e — Closing summary (MANDATORY — PLAIN TEXT ONLY) Your FINAL message for this step MUST be plain Markdown TEXT — never a JSON object, array, or other structured/serialized value. Returning an object makes the run history show "[object Object]" and makes the runner treat the step as FAILED, which triggers a wasteful duplicate re-run of the whole automation. Do NOT return data; type the status block below literally as your final text response: ## Step 6 complete - Rename: - PDF: - Share link: - Email: Never end this step with an empty or internal-only turn. If for any reason you have nothing else to add, still output the status block above as plain text. At an absolute minimum, output the single line: `Step 6 finished without further detail.`
